checks.finding: No material ambiguity prevents a task author from identifying the shell HMAC data-boundary fix and its intended fail-closed behavior.
The requirements, non-goals, workflows, and acceptance criteria state a consistent scope and consent rule.

REQ-002 requires nonce, TTL, and repository binding to be valid before consent, but AC-002 tests only a wholly valid real-HMAC token and AC-003 tests a post-signing field alteration, which is rejected by the invalidated signature. No acceptance test requires denial of a correctly signed token whose nonce, TTL, or repository binding is independently invalid. A verifier therefore cannot determine that these existing fail-closed checks remain effective after the helper change; this is a Phase 1 coverage gap for the stated security boundary, not an implementation-detail request.
The stated python3 runtime and existing PowerShell parity assumptions are sufficiently bounded for later design and task decomposition.
The consent boundary is explicit: only a complete valid token may select sudo consent, and all other outcomes follow the denial path before bundle creation.

REQ-005 requires preserving equivalent PowerShell behavior and documenting implementation-specific safety differences, yet AC-005 and TEST-005 provide neither the expected parity outcomes nor a documentation target. A task author and verifier cannot decide what PowerShell evidence or documentation artifact completes REQ-005; this is a missing observable acceptance surface, not a request for implementation design.
